model_narrative: We assign a collection of items to a set of resources. For each item we decide which single resource will handle it, and we also track the total cost that results from all the assignments we make. Every resource has a limited amount of capacity, and handling an item consumes some of the resource's capacity and incurs a cost that both depend on which resource is chosen. The objective is to minimize the total assignment cost.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the set of resources (agents) available to carry out work

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the set of items (tasks) that each have to be carried out

params.name: a
params.doc: the amount of a resource's capacity consumed when a given item is handled by that resource, in capacity units

params.name: f
params.doc: the cost incurred when a given item is assigned to a given resource, in cost units

params.name: b
params.doc: the total capacity available at each resource, in capacity units

vars.name: x
vars.doc: assignment indicator; equals 1 if the item is assigned to the resource and 0 otherwise

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total cost of all assignments, in cost units

constraints.name: a[i,
constraints.intent: Each resource has a limited amount of capacity, and handling an item draws on that resource's capacity by an amount that depends on the item. For each resource, the total capacity consumed by the items assigned to it must not exceed the capacity available at that resource.

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Every item has to be handled, and no item may be split across resources. For each item, it must be assigned to exactly one resource.

constraints.name: z
constraints.intent: The total assignment cost accounts for every assignment that is made. Set the total cost equal to the sum over all items and resources of the cost of assigning that item to that resource whenever the assignment is in effect.

query: Resource r1 is shouldering three items in the current plan and we'd like to keep any single resource from getting too crowded. If we say r1 can host at most 2 items, what total cost does that scenario land at?
expected_pyomo: model.r1_max2 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['r1', j] for j in model.j) <= 2)

query: Resource r2 is only handling a single item in the optimal plan, which looks like wasted capacity. Couldn't we make it pull its weight and carry at least two items? What's the tradeoff the model is making by leaving it nearly idle?
expected_pyomo: model.r2_min2 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['r2', j] for j in model.j) >= 2)
